title: Apple sues OpenAI for alleged theft of trade secrets and hardware
summary: The lawsuit alleges that OpenAI orchestrated a campaign to get former Apple employees to reveal confidential information about chips.

title: Meta removes its AI-generated image tool on Instagram after criticism
summary: The feature allowed users to create images from user posts and was removed due to privacy concerns.

title: Microsoft loses lawsuit in Brazil for failing to restore hacked Xbox account
summary: A Brazilian court ordered Microsoft to return the account with all games and pay $400 in compensation.

title: SK Hynix warns 2027 will be the worst year for memory shortage, with crisis until 2030
summary: The company's CEO forecast a severe contraction in memory chip supply that will last for years.

title: AMD Radeon RX 9070 GRE drops below $500
summary: The graphics card reduces its price by 9% to compete in the mid-range market just one month after its launch.

title: NVIDIA Blackwell GPU temperature sensor remains accessible via internal diagnostic tools
summary: It was detected that the RTX 5070 Ti reaches 107°C and reduces its performance due to poor thermal paste application.

title: Microsoft's emissions increase 25% due to AI data center expansion
summary: The company faces challenges in meeting its 2030 sustainability goal while growing its AI infrastructure.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 with Sol, Terra, and Luna models for public use
summary: The Sol Ultra model solved a 50-year-old math problem in less than an hour and autonomously trained the Luna model.

title: OpenAI presents ChatGPT Work and announces the closure of the Atlas browser
summary: The company acknowledges initial usability and cost issues and is working to resolve them.

title: U.S. Government Efficiency Department (DOGE) officially closes
summary: The U.S. government cost-cutting initiative comes to an end after months of operation.

title: European Parliament approves Chat Control 1.0 through the back door
summary: The controversial digital communication surveillance measure makes its way into European legislation.

title: Google opposes website blocking in Europe while the US advances anti-piracy measures
summary: The company criticizes proposals to block pages for copyright infringement in the European Union.

title: Terrorist groups use AI chatbots to plan attacks and develop weapons
summary: According to a report, extremist organizations are taking advantage of leading AI assistants for offensive purposes.

title: Ransomware negotiator hired to represent victims actually worked for the attackers
summary: The man was sentenced to six years in prison for betraying the victims he was supposed to defend.

title: US cybersecurity agency CISA admits it had to develop its response plan during an incident
summary: The CISA director revealed they lacked a prepared incident manual and developed it on the fly.

title: Fake DNS scanner on GitHub distributed malware through more than 200 repositories
summary: The 'Muck and Load' operation published 700 malicious modules since January, infecting developers.

title: John Deere loses total control over tractor repairs and will be under supervision for 10 years
summary: The company must allow independent repairs under an agreement that limits its monopoly on maintenance.

title: Chinese probe arrives at asteroid that baffled astronomers and sends first image
summary: China has reached an asteroid considered strange, and the first photos could change current theories.

title: Race for PS5 emulation on PC begins ahead of GTA 6 release
summary: Some 2D games already work on PC via emulation, in anticipation of the next big title.

title: Switzerland discovers snow is unexpected ally of solar panels installed at 2,500 meters
summary: The reflection of snow increases the energy production of solar panels in high mountains.
